recommended_strategy.fresh_bootstrap_track: Create new schema-owned baseline chains after cutover; do not reuse legacy public-schema bootstrap as canonical for empty environments.
recommended_strategy.upgrade_track: Use one-time offline schema move SQL plus schema-aware env flip; do not edit already applied V6/V8 migrations.
why: Applied Flyway versioned migrations must remain immutable.
Current runtime already mitigates active admin role assignment ownership in permission-service.
Fresh bootstrap and in-place upgrade need different safety strategies.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Breaks Flyway checksum on existing environments.
Adds infra scope before schema ownership debt is closed.

invariants: No in-place edit on already applied V6/V8 user-service migrations.
Cutover runs with business services stopped; postgres stays up.
Permission bootstrap remains source of truth for default ADMIN role assignment.
Object inventory and Flyway history evidence are captured before and after cutover.
Rollback must be possible without rewriting historical migration files.

phases.goal: Freeze current runtime state and capture rollback evidence.
steps: Record table inventory, sequence inventory and Flyway history tables.
Capture current service health, Eureka registry and gateway smoke evidence.
Stop api-gateway, auth-service, user-service, permission-service, variant-service and core-data-service in controlled order.

phases.goal: Define clean schema-owned bootstrap chains for empty environments.
steps: Create new canonical bootstrap chains per service for target schema ownership.
User-service bootstrap chain must own only users, user_audit_events and user_notification_preferences.
Permission-service bootstrap chain must own roles, role_permissions, user_role_assignments, scopes, user_permission_scope, audit tables and default admin assignment bootstrap.
Mark legacy public-schema bootstrap chain as legacy-only in docs and rollout notes.

phases.goal: Move existing public objects into target service schemas without touching applied versioned migrations.
steps: Create target schemas if missing.
Move auth-service tables and sequences to auth_service.
Move variant-service tables and sequences to variant_service.
Move core-data-service tables and sequences to core_data_service.
Move permission-service tables and sequences to permission_service.
Move user-service tables and sequences to user_service.
Ensure cross-schema foreign keys and defaults remain valid after SET SCHEMA operations.

phases.goal: Deploy schema-aware configuration in an isolated rehearsal environment and verify runtime behavior.
steps: Set AUTH_DB_SCHEMA, USER_DB_SCHEMA, PERMISSION_DB_SCHEMA, VARIANT_DB_SCHEMA and CORE_DATA_DB_SCHEMA to target schema names.
Start discovery-server, then permission-service, auth-service, user-service, variant-service, core-data-service and api-gateway.
Validate actuator health, Eureka registration, gateway 401 smoke and default admin role assignments.
Run Flyway info/validate checks for auth-service, user-service, permission-service, variant-service and core-data-service.

phases.goal: Execute offline schema move against the live users database and flip runtime configuration in a controlled maintenance window.
steps: Capture pre-cutover runtime lock snapshot from the live stack.
Stop business services while leaving PostgreSQL available.
Apply 02-offline-service-schema-cutover.sql against the live users database.
Flip live AUTH_DB_SCHEMA, USER_DB_SCHEMA, PERMISSION_DB_SCHEMA, VARIANT_DB_SCHEMA and CORE_DATA_DB_SCHEMA values to target schema names.
Start services in discovery -> permission -> user -> auth -> variant -> core-data -> api-gateway order.
Re-run health, Eureka, gateway 401 smoke, admin assignment and Flyway validate evidence collection against the live stack.

notes: Move in same offline window as user-service tables because user_role_assignments references users.
Default admin role assignment bootstrap remains enabled during first post-cutover start.
User-service and permission-service cutover must be treated as a coordinated pair.
Legacy V6/V8 migrations remain historical evidence only; ownership behavior comes from permission-service bootstrap.

validation_gates: All backend services healthy after schema flip.
Eureka lists AUTH-SERVICE, USER-SERVICE, PERMISSION-SERVICE, VARIANT-SERVICE and CORE-DATA-SERVICE.
Admin users keep active ADMIN assignment.
All moved tables and sequences appear under target schemas.
Flyway validate succeeds for all schema-aware services.

trigger_conditions: Flyway validate failure after schema flip.
Gateway smoke returns 5xx or registry misses a critical service.
steps: Stop business services again.
Revert schema env values back to public.
Apply reverse SET SCHEMA operations using pre-cutover inventory.
Re-run health, Eureka and gateway smoke on public schema mode.
Preserve cutover failure evidence for follow-up; do not edit historical migrations.
